content.problemStatement:
Chef‚Äôs football team is playing in the **final of a tournament**.
So far:


Chef‚Äôs team has scored **0 goals**
The opponent team has scored **X goals**
There are **Y minutes remaining** in the match
Chef‚Äôs team has a special ability:
**They can score exactly 1 goal every 5 minutes** (if enough time remains).

Your task is to determine **whether Chef‚Äôs team can win the match** before time runs out.

Chef‚Äôs team **wins** if they score **strictly more goals** than the opponent by the end of the match.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecanChefWincanChefWincanChefWin ‚Äì This function checks whether Chef‚Äôs team can win the match.

Parameters
XXX : The number of goals scored by the opponent team.
YYY : The number of minutes remaining in the match.
Return Value
Returns truetruetrue if Chef‚Äôs team can win the match.
Returns falsefalsefalse otherwise.
Constraints
0‚â§X‚â§1000 \leq X \leq 1000‚â§X‚â§100
0‚â§Y‚â§10000 \leq Y \leq 10000‚â§Y‚â§1000

Input Format

A single line containing two integers XXX and YYY.


Output Format

For each test case, print truetruetrue if Chef‚Äôs team can win the match, otherwise print falsefalsefalse.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains two space‚Äëseparated integers X and Y, where X is the opponent's current goals and Y is the minutes remaining.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "true" if Chef's team can finish with strictly more goals than the opponent, otherwise output "false".

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each test case compute floor(Y/5): 10/5=2 >1 => true; 2 >2? false (equal); 14/5=2 >3? false.
Chef can score 0 goals, opponent also 0, need strictly more, so false.

solution.approach: For each test case compute the maximum number of goals Chef can still score: maxGoals = floor(Y / 5) because Chef scores exactly one goal every 5 minutes and cannot score partial goals. Chef wins if and only if maxGoals > X. The algorithm is O(1) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) ‚Äì constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) ‚Äì only a few integer variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
if not data:
    sys.exit()
it = iter(data)
T = int(next(it))
out = []
for _ in range(T):
    X = int(next(it))
    Y = int(next(it))
    max_goals = Y // 5
    out.append('true' if max_goals > X else 'false')
print('\n'.join(out))
